item.title: 我使用这些 chrome 扩展 | I use these chrome extensions
item.description:
阻止 YouTube、Facebook、Twitch 和您喜爱的网站上的广告和弹出窗口。 显然我们不喜欢广告，所以是的。 我知道许多创作者从广告中赚钱，但有时需要。 但有时我感觉很糟糕，所以我关闭了特定网站😌

我总是很好奇这个网站背后的技术是什么。 因此，此扩展程序立即显示任何网站的技术堆栈。 不确定它是否准确，所以我检查了 stackshare.io

什么字体

识别网页字体的最简单方法。 为您提供完整的详细信息，如字体名称、样式等。

我是表情符号的家伙，所以这个扩展非常有助于直接复制并粘贴到输入框中。

滴管

您的眼睛所见过的最漂亮和可定制的 JSON/JSONP 荧光笔。

反应开发者工具

目前我正在使用 React，所以对调试 react 的东西很有帮助。

是的，用于语法校正。 但有时我会忽略它来纠正语法哈哈😁😅

莫兹酒吧

对于 SEO 的东西，网络的 DA/PA 和反向链接的东西。

相似网站

查看任何网站的网站流量和关键指标，包括参与率、流量排名、关键字排名和流量来源。

允许 CORS：访问控制允许来源

轻松将 (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) 规则添加到响应标头。

黑暗阅读器

每个网站的暗模式。 照顾好你的眼睛，使用深色主题进行夜间和日常浏览。

嘿，我在twitter/awwarpit上



原文:

To Block ads and pop-ups on YouTube, Facebook, Twitch, and your favorite websites. Obviously we don't like ads so yes. I know many creators earn from ads but sometimes needed. But sometimes I feel bad so I turn off for particular websites 😌

Wappalyzer - Technology profiler

I'm always curious about what the technology running behind this website. So, this extension instantly reveal the technology stack any website. Not sure it's accurate or not so I checked on stackshare.io

WhatFont

The easiest way to identify fonts on web pages. Gives you complete detail like font name, style and more.

Emoji Keyboard by JoyPixels

I'm emoji guy, so this extension really helpful to just copy and paste directly into the input box.

Eye Dropper

Eye Dropper is open source extension which allows you to pick colors from web pages, color picker and your personal color history.

JSON Viewer

The most beautiful and customizable JSON/JSONP highlighter that your eyes have ever seen.

React Developer Tools

Currently I'm using React so yeah really helpful to debug react stuff.

Redux DevTools

Redux DevTools for debugging application's state changes.

Grammarly for Chrome

Yes for grammer correction. But sometimes I ignore it to correct grammer lol 😁😅

MozBar

For SEO stuff, DA/PA of the web and backlink stuff.

Similarweb

To see website traffic and key metrics for any website, including engagement rate, traffic ranking, keyword ranking and traffic source.

Allow CORS: Access-Control-Allow-Origin

Easily add (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) rule to the response header.

Dark Reader

Dark mode for every website. Take care of your eyes, use dark theme for night and daily browsing.

Hey I'm here on twitter/awwarpit
item.author: soniarpit

item.title: 📱 AWS CDK 101 - 👯 通过 stepfunction 从 S3 获取 JSON | 📱 AWS CDK 101 - 👯 Fetching JSON from S3 through stepfunction
item.description:
如果万一错过了我之前的文章，请通过以下链接找到它。

在本文中，让我们将之前调用 lambda 的一个 step 函数重构为一个可以在事件数据期间使用键从 s3 读取消息数据的函数，该函数使用来自事件数据的完整消息将记录放入 dynamo。 此外，这是我上面提到的上一篇文章的直接后续。

使用 S3 数据的好处 ⌛

这些不是唯一的好处，这只是我刚刚在部署中观察到的。

  • 直接使用 S3 数据将使事件详细信息有效负载始终具有相同的有效负载大小并且非常轻

  • 当一条巨大的消息被泵入 api 网关时，S3 将充当捕获完整消息有效负载的地方，并且只有 s3 对象标识符被推送到事件详细信息中，这样我们就不会将大量消息推送到实际的移动部件中。

  • 这也有助于通过检查格式、数据完整性、转换甚至防病毒扫描来简单地预处理收到的实际消息。

  • 由于我们不再担心数据的大小，因此将事件详细信息泵入阶跃函数现在将更加高效。

  • 同样在这里，我们使用 S3 作为 staging，如果我们需要高性能和高吞吐量，我们可以将其更改为 dynamodb 来存储此 staging 数据。

建设计划🏗️

正如我们之前提到的，我们正在尝试从 s3 中读取 JSON，从我们在之前的文章中改进的消息记录器 lambda 函数中读取该函数以写入 dynamodb。

 import { S3 } from " aws-sdk " ; const s3 = new S3 ();

将 lambda 中的 put 操作重构为辅助函数🎊

我们重构了之前使用的消息记录器功能，以便更好地重用组件，如下所示。

 const dbPut : any = async ( Record : any , msg : any ) => { const dynamo = new DynamoDB (); const crt_time : number = new Date ( msg . createdAt ). getTime (); const putData : PutItemInput = { TableName : process . env . MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME || "" , Item : { messageId : { S : msg . messageId }, createdAt : { N : ` ${ crt_time } ` }, event : { S : msg . event }, }, ReturnConsumedCapacity : " TOTAL " , }; console . log ( " putData " , JSON . stringify ( putData , undefined , 2 )); await dynamo . putItem ( putData ). promise (); };

重构 sendSuccess Helper 🔮

我们还将利用这个机会将现有的 SendTaskSuccess 语句重构为我们可以高效的专用函数。

 const funcSuccess : any = ( res : any , mid : string , token : string ) => { console . log ( " sending success " , { res }); const sendSuccess : StepFunctions . SendTaskSuccessInput = { output : JSON . stringify ({ statusCode : 200 , headers : { " Content-Type " : " text/json " }, putStatus : { messageId : mid , ProcessorResult : res , }, }), taskToken : token , }; const resultStatus = sfn . sendTaskSuccess ( sendSuccess , ( err : any , data : any ) => { if ( err ) console . log ( err , err . stack ); else console . log ( data ); } ); console . log ( " sent success: " , { resultStatus , sendSuccess }); };

重构 sendFailure Helper 🏄

同样，我们还将重构 sendTaskFailure 模块并将其创建为一个新的辅助函数。

 const funcFailure : any = ( err : any , mid : string , token : string ) => { console . log ( " sending failure " , { err }); const sendFailure : StepFunctions . SendTaskFailureInput = { error : JSON . stringify ( err ), cause : JSON . stringify ({ statusCode : 500 , headers : { " Content-Type " : " text/json " }, putStatus : { messageId : mid , ProcessorResult : err , }, }), taskToken : token , }; const resultStatus = sfn . sendTaskFailure ( sendFailure , ( err : any , data : any ) => { if ( err ) console . log ( err , err . stack ); else console . log ( data ); } ); console . log ( " sent failure: " , { resultStatus , sendFailure }); };

授予对 lambda 的读取对象访问权限🍃

在我们之前的文章中，我们已授予对 putObject 的入口处理程序的访问权限，这里我们将授予对记录器函数的访问权限以读取数据，如下所示。

 stgMsgBucket . grantWrite ( eventCounterBus . handler ); stgMsgBucket . grantRead ( messageRecorder );

以下更改将在 lambda 处理程序中使用，以使用event.detail读取每条消息
现在具有存储桶名称和事件密钥的内容。

这里可以发现，桶名和对象键是从消息中提取出来的，用于从S3中检索一个对象，然后使用上面创建的各种辅助函数将其放入dynamodb进行记录。

 await Promise . all ( event . Records . map ( async ( Record : any ) => { console . log ( " Received message: " , JSON . stringify ( event , undefined , 2 )); const msg = JSON . parse ( Record . body ). Record ; const s3Get = await s3 . getObject ({ Bucket : msg . bucket , Key : msg . key , }) . promise (); const data = s3Get . Body ?. toString ( " utf-8 " ); if ( data ) { msg . event = data ; const token = JSON . parse ( Record . body ). MyTaskToken ; await dbPut ( Record , msg ) . then ( async ( data : any ) => { await funcSuccess ( data , msg . messageId , token ); }) . catch ( async ( err : any ) => { await funcFailure ( err , msg . messageId , token ); }); } }) );

一旦我们得到消息，我们将使用消息内容更新消息对象，并使用我们之前折射的dbPut将其插入到 dynamodb 中。

从状态机调用负载中删除事件内容🍭

现在我们可以在整个管道中去掉对实际消息体的使用，这样可以减少在各种调用过程中传输调用所使用的存储空间。

 const sfnTaskPayload = sfn . TaskInput . fromObject ({ MyTaskToken : sfn . JsonPath . taskToken , Record : { " messageId.$ " : " $.id " , " createdAt.$ " : " $.time " , // "event.$": "States.StringToJson($.detail.message)", // "event.$": "$.detail.message", " bucket.$ " : " $.detail.message.bucket " , " key.$ " : " $.detail.message.key " }, });

同样在上篇文章讨论的入口处理函数中如下图

const message = JSON . parse ( event . body ); message . uuid = getUuid (); message . handler = context . awsRequestId ; message . key = `uploads/ ${ message . uuid } .json` ; message . bucket = process . env . BucketName || "" ; console . log ( " Initial request: " , JSON . stringify ( message , undefined , 2 )); delete message . message ; //new line added, since s3 will have the data

集成测试🍁

关键点异步等待🔔

您可能需要在这里学习的一件重要事情是我们如何处理异步等待，首先从 S3 获取然后写入 dynamodb，以及其他异步操作。

初学者在使用 async-await 时很可能会出错，并且当他们糟糕地实现回调时，他们可能会因为执行泄漏而陷入困境。

到那时，下面的跟踪可以帮助您了解问题所在。

我们将向我们的堆栈添加更多连接，并通过创建新结构使其在即将发布的文章中更有用，因此请考虑关注并订阅我的时事通讯。

如果您想☕ 给我买杯咖啡，那就太好了，以帮助我努力。

🔁 Original post at 🔗 Dev Post 🔁 🔗 Dev Post的原始帖子

🔁 Reposted at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber 🔁 在 🔗 dev 转发到@aravindvcyber



原文:

🔰 Beginners new to AWS CDK, please do look at my previous articles one by one in this series.

If in case missed my previous article, do find it with the below links.

🔁 Original previous post at 🔗 Dev Post

🔁 Reposted previous post at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber

In this article, let us refactor one of our previous step functions which invoked lambda which puts records to dynamo using full message from the event data, into one which can read message data from s3 with keys during the event data. Also, this is a direct follow-up to my previous article mentioned above.

Benefits of using S3 data ⌛

These are not the only benefits, it is just only what I just observed in my deployments.

  • Using S3 data directly will make the event detail payload always of the same payload size and very light

  • While a huge message is pumped into the api gateway S3 will act as the place to capture the full message payload and only the s3 object identifier is pushed into the event detail so that we are not pushing a lot of into the actual moving parts.

  • This also helps to simply preprocess the actual message received by checking for the format, data integrity, transformation, and even antivirus scanning.

  • S3 in itself can trigger various event-driven actions asynchronously by its event notifications.

  • Pumping the event details into the step functions will be now much more efficient as we are no longer worried about the size of the data.

  • Also here we have using S3 as staging, if we need high performance and high throughput we can change it to dynamodb for storing this staging data.

Construction Plan 🏗️

As we mentioned earlier we are trying to read a JSON from s3, from our message recorder lambda function which we have refined in our previous articles for writing to dynamodb.

import { S3 } from "aws-sdk"; const s3 = new S3();

Refactoring put operation inside the lambda into a helper function 🎊

We have refactored the previously used message recorder function for better reuse of the components as shown below.


const dbPut: any = async (Record: any, msg: any) => { const dynamo = new DynamoDB(); const crt_time: number = new Date(msg.createdAt).getTime(); const putData: PutItemInput = { TableName: process.env.MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME || "", Item: { messageId: { S: msg.messageId }, createdAt: { N: `${crt_time}` }, event: { S: msg.event }, }, ReturnConsumedCapacity: "TOTAL", }; console.log("putData", JSON.stringify(putData, undefined, 2)); await dynamo.putItem(putData).promise();
};

Refactoring the sendSuccess Helper 🔮

We will also make use of this opportunity to refactor the existing SendTaskSuccess statement into a dedicated function that we could efficiently.


const funcSuccess: any = (res: any, mid: string, token: string) => { console.log("sending success ", { res }); const sendSuccess: StepFunctions.SendTaskSuccessInput = { output: JSON.stringify({ statusCode: 200, headers: { "Content-Type": "text/json" }, putStatus: { messageId: mid, ProcessorResult: res, }, }), taskToken: token, }; const resultStatus = sfn.sendTaskSuccess( sendSuccess, (err: any, data: any) => { if (err) console.log(err, err.stack); else console.log(data); } ); console.log("sent success: ", { resultStatus, sendSuccess });
};

Refractoring the sendFailure Helper 🏄

Similarly, we will also refactor and create sendTaskFailure module into a new helper function.

const funcFailure: any = (err: any, mid: string, token: string) => { console.log("sending failure ", { err }); const sendFailure: StepFunctions.SendTaskFailureInput = { error: JSON.stringify(err), cause: JSON.stringify({ statusCode: 500, headers: { "Content-Type": "text/json" }, putStatus: { messageId: mid, ProcessorResult: err, }, }), taskToken: token, }; const resultStatus = sfn.sendTaskFailure( sendFailure, (err: any, data: any) => { if (err) console.log(err, err.stack); else console.log(data); } ); console.log("sent failure: ", { resultStatus, sendFailure });
};

Granting read Object access to lambda 🍃

In our previous article we have granted access to the entry handler to putObject, here we will grant access to the recorder function to read data as follows.


stgMsgBucket.grantWrite(eventCounterBus.handler);
stgMsgBucket.grantRead(messageRecorder);

Changes inside the lambda 🍌

The below changes will be used inside the lambda handler to read every message using the event.detail
content which now has the bucket name and the key from the event.

Here can find that the bucket name and object key is extracted from the message and used to retrieve an object from S3, then it is put into dynamodb for recording using the various helper functions created above.

await Promise.all( event.Records.map(async (Record: any) => { console.log("Received message:", JSON.stringify(event, undefined, 2)); const msg = JSON.parse(Record.body).Record; const s3Get = await s3 .getObject({ Bucket: msg.bucket, Key: msg.key, }) .promise(); const data = s3Get.Body?.toString("utf-8"); if (data) { msg.event = data; const token = JSON.parse(Record.body).MyTaskToken; await dbPut(Record, msg) .then(async (data: any) => { await funcSuccess(data, msg.messageId, token); }) .catch(async (err: any) => { await funcFailure(err, msg.messageId, token); }); } }) );

Once we get the message, we are updating the message object with the message content and inserting it into dynamodb with the dbPut we have refracted earlier.

Removing the event content from statemachine invocation payload 🍭

Now we can remove the usage of the actual message body across the entire pipeline, which makes it reduce the storage used in transmission call during various invocations call.

const sfnTaskPayload = sfn.TaskInput.fromObject({ MyTaskToken: sfn.JsonPath.taskToken, Record: { "messageId.$": "$.id", "createdAt.$": "$.time", // "event.$": "States.StringToJson($.detail.message)", // "event.$": "$.detail.message", "bucket.$": "$.detail.message.bucket", "key.$": "$.detail.message.key" }, });

Also in the entry handler function discussed in the last article as shown below

 const message = JSON.parse(event.body); message.uuid = getUuid(); message.handler = context.awsRequestId; message.key = `uploads/${message.uuid}.json`; message.bucket = process.env.BucketName || ""; console.log("Initial request:", JSON.stringify(message, undefined, 2)); delete message.message; //new line added, since s3 will have the data

Integration testing 🍁

Keypoint async-await 🔔

One important thing you may have to learn here would be how we have handled the async-await, to fetch from S3 first and then write to dynamodb, among other async operations.

Beginners most likely may go wrong in using async-await and they may mesh up by getting execution leaks when they implemented callbacks poorly.

By then the trace below can help you understand where the problem is present.

We will be adding more connections to our stack and making it more usable in the upcoming articles by creating new constructs, so do consider following and subscribing to my newsletter.

⏭ We have our next article in serverless, do check out

🎉 Thanks for supporting! 🙏

Would be great if you like to ☕ Buy Me a Coffee, to help boost my efforts.

🔁 Original post at 🔗 Dev Post

🔁 Reposted at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber
item.author: aravindvcyber

item.title: 如何使用 Kubernetes 部署 Apache Kafka | How To Deploy Apache Kafka With Kubernetes
item.description:
对于许多组织而言，在 Kubernetes 上部署 Kafka 是一种符合其架构策略的省力方法。

在这篇文章中，我们将探讨在 Kubernetes 上托管 Kafka 的吸引力，并提供两个应用程序的快速入门。 最后，我们将介绍一种与云无关的方法来配置 Kubernetes 以部署 Kafka 及其同级服务。

技术入门

让我们从 Kubernetes 和 Kafka 的快速概述开始。

谷歌于 2003 年开始开发最终成为 Kubernetes (k8s) 的东西。当时，该项目被称为Borg 。 多年来，越来越多的尝试使用 Kubernetes，包括GitHub本身和流行的游戏Pokémon GO 。 将 k8s 引入云开发生命周期提供了几个关键优势：

  • 零停机部署
  • 可扩展性
  • 不可变的基础设施
  • 自愈系统

其中许多好处来自于在 k8s 中使用声明式配置。 为了更改基础设施配置，必须销毁和重建资源，从而强制执行不变性。 此外，如果 k8s 检测到资源偏离了声明的规范，它会尝试重建系统的状态以再次匹配该规范。

对于开发人员来说，使用 k8s 意味着最终结束令人沮丧的午夜部署，您必须放弃一切以直接手动扩展服务或修补生产环境。

阿帕奇卡夫卡

这些消息在每个主题中作为队列排序。 不同地区可以有许多不同的 Kafka 代理。 除了 Kafka 代理之外，另一个名为 Zookeeper 的服务使不同的代理保持同步，并帮助协调主题和消息。

消息在生产者和消费者之间传递，这实际上是云中的服务。 同一个服务既可以是消费者，也可以是来自 Kafka 内部相同或不同主题的消息的生产者。

一个示例用例是在您的应用程序中创建一个新用户。 用户服务发布关于“供应用户”主题的消息。 电子邮件服务使用此关于新用户的消息，然后向他们发送欢迎电子邮件。 用户和电子邮件服务不必直接相互发送消息，但它们各自的作业是异步执行的。

使用 Kubernetes 部署 Kafka

对于我们的迷你项目演练，我们将使用 Minikube 以云中立的方式设置 Kubernetes 和 Kafka，这允许我们在单台机器上运行整个 k8s 集群。 我们安装了以下应用程序：

  • 安装 Minikube 后，我们可以使用 minikube start 命令启动它。 然后，我们可以看到状态：

     $ minikube status minikube type : Control Plane host: Running kubelet: Running apiserver: Running kubeconfig: Configured

    可以在每个提供商（例如AWS 、 GCP或Azure ）的文档中找到设置 Kubernetes 以在您选择的云提供商中运行的说明，但下面列出的 YAML 配置文件应该适用于所有提供商，只需稍作调整用于 IP 地址和相关字段。

    定义 Kafka 命名空间

    首先，我们使用名为00-namespace.yaml的文件定义一个用于部署所有 Kafka 资源的命名空间：

     apiVersion : v1 kind : Namespace metadata : name : " kafka" labels : name : " kafka"

    我们使用kubectl apply -f 00-namespace.yaml应用这个文件。

    我们可以通过运行kubectl get namespaces来测试命名空间是否正确创建，验证 Kafka 是 Minikube 中存在的命名空间。

    部署 Zookeeper

    接下来，我们将 Zookeeper 部署到我们的 k8s 命名空间。 我们创建一个名为01-zookeeper.yaml的文件，其内容如下：

     apiVersion : v1 kind : Service metadata : labels : app : zookeeper-service name : zookeeper-service namespace : kafka spec : type : NodePort ports : - name : zookeeper-port port : 2181 nodePort : 30181 targetPort : 2181 selector : app : zookeeper --- apiVersion : apps/v1 kind : Deployment metadata : labels : app : zookeeper name : zookeeper namespace : kafka spec : replicas : 1 selector : matchLabels : app : zookeeper template : metadata : labels : app : zookeeper spec : containers : - image : wurstmeister/zookeeper imagePullPolicy : IfNotPresent name : zookeeper ports : - containerPort : 2181

    此 YAML 文件中创建了两个资源。 第一个是名为zookeeper-service ，它将使用在名为zookeeper的第二个资源中创建的部署。 部署使用wurstmeister/zookeeper Docker 镜像作为实际的 Zookeeper 二进制文件。 该服务在内部 k8s 网络的一个端口上公开该部署。 在这种情况下，我们使用标准 Zookeeper 端口2181 ，Docker 容器也公开了该端口。

    我们使用以下命令应用此文件： kubectl apply -f 01-zookeeper.yaml 。

    我们可以如下测试成功创建的服务：

     $ kubectl get services -n kafka NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP PORT ( S ) AGE zookeeper-service NodePort 10.100.69.243 2181:30181/TCP 3m4s

    我们看到 Zookeeper 的内部 IP 地址（ 10.100.69.243 ），我们需要告诉代理在哪里监听它。

    部署 Kafka 代理

    最后一步是部署 Kafka 代理。 我们创建一个包含以下内容的02-kafka.yaml文件，将<ZOOKEEPER-INTERNAL-IP>替换为 Zookeeper 上一步中的CLUSTER-IP 。 如果不采取此步骤，代理将无法部署。

     apiVersion : v1 kind : Service metadata : labels : app : kafka-broker name : kafka-service namespace : kafka spec : ports : - port : 9092 selector : app : kafka-broker --- apiVersion : apps/v1 kind : Deployment metadata : labels : app : kafka-broker name : kafka-broker namespace : kafka spec : replicas : 1 selector : matchLabels : app : kafka-broker template : metadata : labels : app : kafka-broker spec : hostname : kafka-broker containers : - env : - name : KAFKA_BROKER_ID value : " 1" - name : KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT value : <ZOOKEEPER-INTERNAL-IP>:2181 - name : KAFKA_LISTENERS value : PLAINTEXT://:9092 - name : KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS value : PLAINTEXT://kafka-broker:9092 image : wurstmeister/kafka imagePullPolicy : IfNotPresent name : kafka-broker ports : - containerPort : 9092

    同样，我们正在为单个 Kafka Broker 创建两个资源——服务和部署。 我们运行kubectl apply -f 02-kafka.yaml 。 我们通过查看命名空间中的 pod 来验证这一点：

     $ kubectl get pods -n kafka NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv 1/1 Running 0 48s zookeeper-55b668879d-xc8vd 1/1 Running 0 35m

    注意02-kafka.yaml中我们为KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS提供值的行。 为了确保 Zookeeper 和 Kafka 可以使用这个主机名（ kafka-broker ）进行通信，我们需要在本地机器上的/etc/hosts文件中添加以下条目：

    测试 Kafka 主题

    为了测试我们是否可以从 Kafka 的主题中发送和检索消息，我们需要为 Kafka 公开一个端口以使其可以从 localhost 访问。 我们运行以下命令来公开一个端口：

     $ kubectl port-forward kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv 9092 -n kafka Forwarding from 127.0.0.1:9092 -> 9092 Forwarding from [ ::1]:9092 -> 9092

    上面的命令kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv引用了我们在上面列出kafka命名空间中的 pod 时看到的 k8s pod。 此命令使该 pod 的端口9092在 Minikube k8s 集群之外的localhost:9092可用。

    为了轻松地从 Kafka 发送和检索消息，我们将使用一个名为KCat （以前称为 Kafkacat）的命令行工具。 要创建一条消息和一个名为test的主题，我们运行以下命令：

     $ echo "hello world!" | kafkacat -P -b localhost:9092 -t test

    该命令应该没有错误地执行，表明生产者与 k8s 中的 Kafka 通信良好。 我们如何查看名为test的队列中当前有哪些消息？ 我们运行以下命令：

     $ kafkacat -C -b localhost:9092 -t test hello world! % Reached end of topic test [ 0] at offset 1

    我们做到了！ 我们已经成功地用 Kubernetes 部署了 Kafka！

    下一步

    对于有兴趣实施实时、事件驱动的架构和系统的组织来说，Kafka 是一个关键的参与者。 使用 Kubernetes 部署 Kafka 是一个很好的开始，但组织还需要弄清楚如何使 Kafka 与现有的 API 生态系统无缝、安全地工作。 他们需要的是工具来处理其 API 整个生命周期的管理和安全性。

    在这些供应商中，我遇到了 Gravitee，它是领先的解决方案之一，它特别专注于帮助组织管理、保护、治理和产品化他们的 API 生态系统——无论他们基于什么协议、服务或样式构建. 它还可以使用 Websocket 等网络友好协议将 Kafka 事件流式传输给消费者。

    结论

    在本文中，我们讨论了 Kafka 如何帮助编排微服务架构，因为它是一个中枢神经系统，在许多不同的服务之间传递消息。 为了部署 Kafka，我们研究了 Kubernetes，这是一个强大的容器编排平台，您可以在本地（使用 Minikube）或使用云提供商在生产环境中运行。 最后，我们演示了如何使用 Minikube 设置本地 Kubernetes 集群，部署 Kafka，然后使用 KCat 验证部署和配置是否成功。

    部署愉快！



原文:

Kafka is the de facto event store and distributed message broker solution for large microservice architecture systems. Kubernetes is the industry standard for orchestrating containerized services. For many organizations, deploying Kafka on Kubernetes is a low-effort approach that fits within their architecture strategy.

In this post, we'll look at the appeal of hosting Kafka on Kubernetes, providing a quick primer on both applications. Finally, we'll walk through a cloud-agnostic method to configure Kubernetes for deploying Kafka and its sibling services.

A Primer on the Tech

Let's start with a quick overview of Kubernetes and Kafka.

Kubernetes

Google started developing what eventually became Kubernetes (k8s) in 2003. At the time, the project was known as Borg. In 2014, Google released k8s as an open-source project on Github, and k8s quickly picked up partners through Microsoft, Red Hat, and Docker. Over the years, more and more endeavors used Kubernetes, including GitHub itself and the popular game, Pokémon GO. In 2022, we see k8s usage growing in the AI/ML space and with an increasing emphasis on security.

The introduction of k8s into the cloud development lifecycle provided several key benefits:

  • Zero downtime deployments
  • Scalability
  • Immutable infrastructure
  • Self-healing systems

Many of these benefits come from the use of declarative configuration in k8s. In order to change an infrastructure configuration, resources must be destroyed and rebuilt, thereby enforcing immutability. In addition, if k8s detects resources that have drifted out of the declared specification, it attempts to rebuild the state of the system to match that specification again.

For developers, using k8s means finally putting an end to the frustrating midnight deployments where you have to drop everything to scale up services or patch production environments directly by hand.

Apache Kafka

Kafka is an open-source distributed stream processing tool. Kafka allows for multiple "producers" to add messages (key-value pairs) to "topics". These messages are ordered in each topic as a queue. "Consumers" subscribe to the topic and can retrieve messages in the order they arrived in the queue.

Kafka is hosted on a server typically called a "broker." There can be many different Kafka brokers in different regions. In addition to Kafka brokers, another service named Zookeeper keeps different brokers in sync and helps coordinate topics and messages.

The brilliance of Kafka is that it can handle hundreds of thousands of messages a second while being distributed and at a relatively cheap cost per MB.

Kafka often occupies a spot akin to the "central nervous system" of a microservice architecture. Messages are passed along between producers and consumers, which in reality, are services inside your cloud. The same service could both be a consumer and a producer of messages from the same or different topics inside Kafka.

An example use case is creating a new user in your application. The User service publishes a message on a "Provision User" topic. The Email service consumes this message about a new user and then sends a welcome email to them. The User and Email services did not have to directly message each other, but their respective jobs were executed asynchronously.

Deploying Kafka With Kubernetes

For our mini project walkthrough, we'll set up Kubernetes and Kafka in a cloud-neutral way using Minikube, which allows us to run an entire k8s cluster on a single machine. We installed the following applications:

  • Minikube version v1.25.2
  • Kubectl client version 1.23.3

Minikube Setup

With Minikube installed, we can start it with the minikube start command. Then, we can see the status:

$ minikube status minikube type: Control Plane
host: Running
kubelet: Running
apiserver: Running
kubeconfig: Configured

Instructions for setting up Kubernetes to run in your cloud provider of choice can be found in the documentation for each provider (for example, AWS, GCP, or Azure), but the YAML configuration files listed below should work across all providers, with minor adjustments for IP addresses and related fields.

Defining a Kafka Namespace

First, we define a namespace for deploying all Kafka resources, using a file named 00-namespace.yaml:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata: name: "kafka" labels: name: "kafka"

We apply this file using kubectl apply -f 00-namespace.yaml.

We can test that the namespace was created correctly by running kubectl get namespaces, verifying that Kafka is a namespace present in Minikube.

Deploying Zookeeper

Next, we deploy Zookeeper to our k8s namespace. We create a file name 01-zookeeper.yaml with the following contents:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: labels: app: zookeeper-service name: zookeeper-service namespace: kafka
spec: type: NodePort ports: - name: zookeeper-port port: 2181 nodePort: 30181 targetPort: 2181 selector: app: zookeeper
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata: labels: app: zookeeper name: zookeeper namespace: kafka
spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: zookeeper template: metadata: labels: app: zookeeper spec: containers: - image: wurstmeister/zookeeper imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent name: zookeeper ports: - containerPort: 2181

There are two resources created in this YAML file. The first is the service called zookeeper-service, which will use the deployment created in the second resource named zookeeper. The deployment uses the wurstmeister/zookeeper Docker image for the actual Zookeeper binary. The service exposes that deployment on a port on the internal k8s network. In this case, we use the standard Zookeeper port of 2181, which the Docker container also exposes.

We apply this file with the following command: kubectl apply -f 01-zookeeper.yaml.

We can test for the successfully created service as follows:

$ kubectl get services -n kafka
NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP PORT(S)         AGE
zookeeper-service  NodePort  10.100.69.243  2181:30181/TCP  3m4s

We see the internal IP address of Zookeeper (10.100.69.243), which we’ll need to tell the broker where to listen for it.

Deploying a Kafka Broker

The last step is to deploy a Kafka broker. We create a 02-kafka.yaml file with the following contents, be we replace <ZOOKEEPER-INTERNAL-IP> with the CLUSTER-IP from the previous step for Zookeeper. The broker will fail to deploy if this step is not taken.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: labels: app: kafka-broker name: kafka-service namespace: kafka
spec: ports: - port: 9092 selector: app: kafka-broker
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata: labels: app: kafka-broker name: kafka-broker namespace: kafka
spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: kafka-broker template: metadata: labels: app: kafka-broker spec: hostname: kafka-broker containers: - env: - name: KAFKA_BROKER_ID value: "1" - name: KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT value: <ZOOKEEPER-INTERNAL-IP>:2181 - name: KAFKA_LISTENERS value: PLAINTEXT://:9092 - name: KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS value: PLAINTEXT://kafka-broker:9092 image: wurstmeister/kafka imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent name: kafka-broker ports: - containerPort: 9092

Again, we are creating two resources — service and deployment — for a single Kafka Broker. We run kubectl apply -f 02-kafka.yaml. We verify this by seeing the pods in our namespace:

$ kubectl get pods -n kafka
NAME                            READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv   1/1     Running   0          48s
zookeeper-55b668879d-xc8vd      1/1     Running   0          35m

The Kafka Broker pod might take a minute to move from ContainerCreating status to Running status.

Notice the line in 02-kafka.yaml where we provide a value for KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS. To ensure that Zookeeper and Kafka can communicate by using this hostname (kafka-broker), we need to add the following entry to the /etc/hosts file on our local machine:

Testing Kafka Topics

In order to test that we can send and retrieve messages from a topic in Kafka, we will need to expose a port for Kafka to make it accessible from localhost. We run the following command to expose a port:

$ kubectl port-forward kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv 9092 -n kafka
Forwarding from 127.0.0.1:9092 -> 9092
Forwarding from [::1]:9092 -> 9092

The above command kafka-broker-5c55f544d4-hrgnv references the k8s pod that we saw above when we listed the pods in our kafka namespace. This command makes the port 9092 of that pod available outside of the Minikube k8s cluster at localhost:9092.

To easily send and retrieve messages from Kafka, we’ll use a command-line tool named KCat (formerly Kafkacat). To create a message and a topic named test, we run the following command:

$ echo "hello world!" | kafkacat -P -b localhost:9092 -t test

The command should execute without errors, indicating that producers are communicating fine with Kafka in k8s. How do we see what messages are currently on the queue named test? We run the following command:

$ kafkacat -C -b localhost:9092 -t test hello world!
% Reached end of topic test [0] at offset 1

We did it! We have successfully deployed Kafka with Kubernetes!

Next Steps

Kafka is a key player for organizations that are interested in implementing real-time, event-driven architectures and systems. Deploying Kafka with Kubernetes is a great start, but organizations will also need to figure out how to make Kafka work seamlessly and securely with their existing API ecosystems. What they’ll need are tools to handle management and security for the entire lifecycle of their APIs.

Among the providers out there, I came across Gravitee, one of the leading solutions that’s particularly focused on helping organizations manage, secure, govern, and productize their API ecosystems — no matter what protocols, services, or styles they’re building on top of. Gravitee even has a Kafka connector that ingests data by exposing endpoints that transform requests into messages that can then be published to your Kafka topic. It can also stream Kafka events to consumers with web-friendly protocols like Websocket.

Conclusion

In this article, we’ve talked about how Kafka helps choreograph microservice architectures by being a central nervous system relaying messages to and from many different services. For deploying Kafka, we’ve looked at Kubernetes, a powerful container orchestration platform that you can run locally (with Minikube) or in production environments with cloud providers. Lastly, we demonstrated how to use Minikube to set up a local Kubernetes cluster, deploy Kafka, and then verify a successful deployment and configuration using KCat.

Happy deploying!
item.author: alvinslee

item.title: 不要将 100vh 用于移动响应 | Don't use 100vh for mobile responsive
item.description:
通常，我们使用height:100vh进行全屏布局，这很容易获得更好的设计。

例子

.content { height : 100vh ; }

但是当我们在实际设备上测试我们的设计时，我们会遇到几个问题：

  • 大多数移动设备上的 Chrome 和 Firefox 浏览器在顶部都有一个 UI（地址栏等）。
  • 在 Safari 上，更棘手的地址栏位于底部。
  • 不同的浏览器有不同大小的视口
  • 移动设备计算浏览器视口为（顶栏 + 文档 + 底栏）= 100vh
  • 使用 100vh 将整个文档填充到页面

问题

已检测到滚动条问题。 糟糕的用户流和难以浏览的内容。

注意：我还在 safari 上测试过这个问题，这会导致更糟糕的用户流量

解决方案

使用JS检测app的高度

使用window.innerheight属性设置页面的高度（使用 javascript）。

 const documentHeight = () => { const doc = document . documentElement doc . style . setProperty ( ' --doc-height ' , ` ${ window . innerHeight } px` ) } window . addEventListener ( ' resize ' , documentHeight ) documentHeight ()

使用，CSS 变量

:root { --doc-height : 100% ; } html , body { padding : 0 ; margin : 0 ; height : 100vh ; /* fallback for Js load */ height : var ( --doc-height ); }

在这里，documentHeight 函数设置新的样式属性 var('--doc-height') 并包含当前窗口高度。

最终结果

注意：现在没有任何垂直的额外滚动条出现在 Safari 上也没有问题。 结论
所以，我建议你尝试一下你的项目并享受它！

如果您有任何问题或疑问，请随时分享您的想法和意见，并给我留言。

直到那时，
继续黑客攻击，干杯



原文:

Generally, we use height:100vh for fullscreen layout which is easy hack and convenient way to get better design.

Example 

.content { height: 100vh;
}

But when we test our design on actual device, we encounter several issues:

  • Mostly Chrome and Firefox browsers on mobile have got a UI (address bar etc) at the top.
  • On Safari it get's more tricky address bar is at the bottom.
  • Different browsers have different sized viewports
  • Mobile devices calc browser viewport as (top bar + document + bottom bar) = 100vh
  • Whole document is filled to the page using 100vh

Problems

Scrollbar issues has been detected. Bad user flow and difficult to navigate the content.

Note: I have also tested this issues on safari which makes more bad user flow

Solutions

Detect the height of the app by using JS

Setting the height of the page (using javascript) with the window.innerheight property.

const documentHeight = () => { const doc = document.documentElement doc.style.setProperty('--doc-height', `${window.innerHeight}px`)
}
window.addEventListener(‘resize’, documentHeight)
documentHeight()

Using, CSS Variable 

:root { --doc-height: 100%;
} html,
body { padding: 0; margin: 0; height: 100vh; /* fallback for Js load */ height: var(--doc-height);
}

Here, documentHeight function sets new style property var('--doc-height') and includes current window height.

Final Results

Chrome Browser

Note: There is no any vertical extra scrollbar is appearing now also no issues on Safari too. The bottom address bar of safari is always on the bottom which makes good user flow to the website

Conclusion
👏👏 By coming this far I hope you can solve the mobile devices viewport issues. So, I suggest you give it a try on your project and enjoy it!

Feel free to share your thoughts and opinions and leave me a comment if you have any problems or questions.

Till then,
Keep on Hacking, Cheers

item.title: Pod 生命周期 | Pod life-cycle
item.description:
一个豆荚

换句话说，一个 pod 永远不会自我修复，当一个 pod 被安排在节点上然后失败时，它就会被删除，因此如果命名空间或节点级别上的资源不足，一个 pod 就无法生存。

待定状态

如果 pod 当前处于挂起状态。 表示该 pod 已被集群或 Kubernetes 系统接受。 在容器启动之前，pod 将保持挂起状态。
运行状态：

成功状态

所有容器都以状态 0 退出或终止，之后标记为成功。
失败状态：

这是所有容器都退出或终止但一个或多个容器以非零状态返回时的情况。

未知状态

容器无法启动，但尝试一次又一次地重新启动。 由于某些原因，kubelet 无法获取 pod 的状态。



原文:

A pod is the atomic unit of scheduling and is a run-time entity. A pod

Pods contain one or more containers, It is transient by nature therefore if a pod fails, Kubernetes can automatically create a new replica of that pod to continue operations.

A pod never heal itself in other words, when a pod get scheduled on the node and then fails, It then gets deleted therefore a pod doesn’t survive if there is a lack of resources either on the namespace or the node level.

Pending State

If a pod is currently in the pending state. It means that the pod has been accepted by the cluster or Kubernetes system. The pod remains in the pending state until the containers are started.
Running State :

All the containers in the pod created. The pod has been schedule on a node however most importantly at least one of the containers define is still running.

Succeeded State

The pod has performed the task. All the container(s) exited or terminated with status 0 and after that marked as Succeeded.
Failed State :

This is the situation when all the containers are exited or terminated but one or more container(s) have returned back with a non-zero status.

Unknown State

The container fails to start but tried to restart again and again. The kubelet is unable to get the state of the pod due to some reasons.

item.title: 与 Rapid Miner 的相关性 | Correlation With Rapid Miner
item.description:
尤其是最近取暖油市场价格的波动，加上每个家庭取暖油订单的规模差异很大，这让莎拉感到担忧。

她需要了解哪些因素与取暖油的使用有关，以及她如何利用这些因素的知识来更好地管理她的库存和预期的需求。

相关性是数据集中属性之间的关系有多强的统计量度。

数据集包括：

绝缘

温度

取暖油

入住人数

平均年龄

家居尺寸

我们的数据集

应用相关矩阵

相关结果

所有介于 0 和 1 之间的相关系数代表正相关

虽然 0 和 -1 之间的所有系数都是**负相关**

取暖油和绝缘等级呈正相关，这意味着随着取暖油需求的增加，绝缘等级也会增加，反之亦然。

温度和绝缘等级之间存在负相关。

这意味着温度升高，绝缘等级下降，反之亦然。

结论

我们数据中两个最相关的属性是： 取暖油和平均年龄

以下是莎拉可以采取的一些行动来做出她的决定

  1. 删除 Number Of occupants 属性
  2. 调查家庭绝缘的作用
  3. 在数据集中添加更大的粒度
  4. 向数据集添加附加属性


原文:

Sarah is a regional sales manager for a nationwide supplier of fossil fuels for home heating.
Recent volatility in market prices for heating oil specifically, coupled with wide variability in the size of each order for home heating oil, has Sarah concerned.

She needs to understand what factors are related to heating oil usage, and how she might use the knowledge of such factors to better manage her inventory and anticipated demands.

Correlation is a statistical measure of how strong the relationships are between attributes in a dataset.

The dataset consists of;

Insulation

Temperature

Heating Oil

Number Of Occupants

Average Age

Home Size

Our Dataset

Applying Correlation Matrix

Correlation Results

All correlation coefficients between 0 and 1 represents Positive Correlations

While all coefficients between 0 and -1 are** Negative Correlations**

There is a positive correlation with Heating oil and Insulation Rating which means as the demand of heating oil rises so will insulation rating and vice versa.

There is a Negative Correlation between Temperature and Insulation rating.

Which means has temperature rises, insulation rating falls and vice versa.

Conclusion

The 2 most strongly correlated attributes in our data are; Heating oil and Average Age

Here are few actions Sarah can take in order to make her decisions

  1. Dropping the Number Of occupants attribute
  2. Investigate the role of home insulation
  3. Adding greater granularity in the dataset
  4. Adding additional attribute to the dataset

item.title: 关于 10 年内科技企业将如何变化的 3 个预测 | 3 Predictions About How Technology Businesses Will Change In 10 Years
item.description:
本文是由 Lewis Dowling 为Dev Interrupted撰写的。

在过去的十年中，技术变得更加复杂。 快点。 更小。 更有力。 但是，快速发展的不仅仅是我们的技术。 我们的文化、态度和政治也在发生变化。

那么未来十年会是什么样子呢？ 企业如何改变以跟上技术的步伐？ 我们与 Redpoint Ventures 的董事总经理 Jason Warner 进行了交谈，以了解他对此事的看法。

近年来发生了很多大的变化。 人们在家工作。 政治局势高度紧张。 几乎每台设备都会收集关于我们的数据。 在所有这些情况下，安全性都很重要。 保护我们的企业、我们的国家机密和我们的私人生活。

这一切都导致了一个不可避免的结论。 保护数据将成为政府、企业和个人的首要关注点。 因为，随着世界变得更加数字化，我们将看到越来越多的网络攻击。

我认为，如果公司现在没有首席合规官，他们将来可能会有，”杰森说。 但更重要的是，这就像理解我们不再是一个叫做美国的微型地理单位。” 标准的商业模式已经过时。 过去，科技公司卖软件，他们把软件给客户，这就是交易的结束。 但现在，更多的是建立社区和与客户定期互动。 这是关于订阅、定期付款甚至是捐赠模式，在 Twitch 等流行平台上可以看到。 软件不再是产品。 这是一项服务。

但如今几乎每家公司都是科技公司。 看看出租车行业发生了什么。 该模型已经完全改变，仅仅是因为技术已经发展。 旧模式不会完全消失，但随着新技术的普及，我们会看到越来越多的行业进入订阅模式。

必须有人先找到你，”杰森解释道。 随着每一项新的创新，我们对所使用的硬件提出了更高的要求。 我们的软件越先进，我们的硬件就必须越强大。 但目前，大多数公司依靠国际贸易来制造关键部件。 随着紧张局势的加剧，我们很可能会看到公司开始将这些资源带到离家更近的地方，并在此过程中保护他们的供应链。

观看完整的采访

如果您对 Jason 对未来十年的其他看法以及社会面临的挑战感兴趣，您可以在我们的网站上观看完整的播客。

渴望顶级软件工程内容？ 需要一些关于如何管理团队的好技巧？ 本文的灵感来自Dev Interrupted - 工程领导者的首选播客。

立即收听并订阅您选择的流媒体服务。



原文:

This article was written for Dev Interrupted by Lewis Dowling.

Over the last ten years, technology has become more sophisticated. Faster. Smaller. More powerful. But it isn’t just our technology that’s evolving at a rapid pace. Our culture, attitudes and politics are all changing, too.

So what could the next ten years look like? How might businesses change to keep up with technology? We spoke with Jason Warner, managing director at Redpoint Ventures, to get his thoughts on the matter.

“Ten years is an interestingly long, but also short time horizon,” Jason explained. “It’s likely we’ll see a complete company cycle, maybe two macroeconomic cycles.” -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 10:29

1. Organizations will invest more in compliance and security

There have been a lot of large changes in recent years. People are working from home. Political tensions are high. And almost every device collects data about us. In all these cases, security is important. Securing our businesses, our national secrets, and our private lives.

It all leads to an inevitable conclusion. Jason believes that chief compliance officers will become commonplace, even in small companies. Protecting data is going to become a primary concern, for governments, businesses and people. Because, as the world gets more digital, we’re going to see more and more cyber attacks.

“Trends that I see happening are an increased awareness and investment in things like compliance and security. I think that if companies don’t have a chief compliance officer now, they likely will in the future,” Jason said. “I think it’s interesting when you see the geopolitical environment of how we might have to invest in more sophisticated tooling for national security. But more than that, it’s like understanding that we’re no longer a single micro-geo unit called the United States.” -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 11:03

2. Companies will focus on loyalty and subscriptions over one-off sales

The standard business model is outdated. In the past, technology companies sold software, they gave customers the software and that was the end of the transaction. But now, it’s more about building communities and regular interaction with your customers. It’s about subscriptions, regular payments or even donation models, seen on popular platforms like Twitch. Software isn’t a product any more. It’s a service.

But almost every company these days is a technology company. Just look at what’s happened to the taxi industry. The model has completely changed, simply because the technology has evolved. The old model won’t completely disappear, but we’ll see more and more industries move into a subscription model as new technology takes over.

“Selling is about adoption first and selling second. Someone’s got to reach for you first,” Jason explained. “Then, they’re going to find a value problem, then they’re going to want to give you money if they’re finding utility out of you.” -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast at 11:21

3. Hardware is, and always will be, just as important as software

With every new innovation, we place more demands on the hardware we’re using. The more advanced our software becomes, the more powerful our hardware must be. But right now, most companies rely on international trade to build key components. With tensions rising, it’s likely that we’ll see companies begin to bring these resources closer to home, securing their supply chain in the process.

“There’s interestingly a lot more emphasis on investing in hardware again,” Jason said. “And America in particular owning its hardware manufacturing, which I think is obviously good.” -On the Dev Interrupted Podcast 11:41

Watch the full interview

If you’re interested in what else Jason had to say about the next ten years, and what challenges society faces, you can watch the full podcast on our site.

Starved for top-level software engineering content? Need some good tips on how to manage your team? This article is inspired by Dev Interrupted - the go-to podcast for engineering leaders.

Dev Interrupted features expert guests from around the world to explore strategy and day-to-day topics ranging from dev team metrics to accelerating delivery. With new guests every week from Google to small startups, the Dev Interrupted Podcast is a fresh look at the world of software engineering and engineering management.

item.title: 无代码支付：如何使用带有定制 NFC 标签的 Stripe | No-code payments: How to use Stripe with customized NFC tags
item.description:
付款链接就是：链接！ 在任何可以共享 URL 的地方，您都可以创造与付费客户建立联系的机会。 这篇博文分享了一些使用 NFC 标签或二维码进行面对面支付的想法。

当今市场上几乎所有的智能手机设备都兼容 NFC。

我们将介绍的步骤

  1. 在 Stripe Dashboard 中创建（或选择）产品
  2. 为该产品创建付款链接
  3. 复制该支付链接的 URL，并将其写入 QR 码和 NFC 标签
  4. 当有人用智能手机敲打标签时，支付工作流程就开始了！

背景

几周前，我去基韦斯特度假。 一天下午，我在安静的市区走来走去，偶然发现了一所房子前面的一个小亭子。 乍一看，它有点像一个免费的小图书馆——它是一个简单的小柜子，里面有一个有机玻璃窗和几个小书架。 每个架子上都有一张设计精美的明信片，由当地艺术家设计和印刷。

明信片的价格很简单——每张 5 美元，或者 5 块 20 美元。 这是一个基于信任的系统：明信片处于解锁状态，要付款，我被要求通过 Venmo 提交付款。 我抓起一沓明信片，拿出手机，按照售货亭前面的说明寄给艺术家 20 美元。

很酷！ 我很高兴，我相信艺术家也很高兴！ 但是——这次经历让我的创业齿轮转动了……我不禁觉得卖家有很多机会在这里做得更好：

  • 我必须按照一系列步骤付款，使用特定的移动支付应用程序向他们发送付款。 如果我以前从未使用过该应用程序，这会产生摩擦。 下载一个应用程序，设置一个帐户，输入我的信用卡号等，需要做很多工作
  • 在我拿走我的购买后，艺术家失去了再次向我出售的机会，除非我碰巧经过同一个售货亭并且他们已经改变了库存。 现在我已经从他们那里买过一次东西，我更有可能再做一次。 如果卖家有办法在销售后与我联系怎么办？

我们可以使用 Stripe 的一些工具来改进这个购买过程，而无需编写任何代码！ 为此，我们将使用支付链接- 一种创建 URL 的便捷方式，可将访问者引导至您的任何产品的 Stripe 支付工作流程。 这意味着我们的买家无需手动输入电话号码、用户名或价格即可提交付款，从而减少出错的余地。

先决条件 - 设置您的 Stripe 帐户并创建产品

本教程假设您已经使用 Stripe 设置了一个（免费！）帐户。 如果您的企业还没有一个，您只需几分钟即可在此处注册。 完成此操作后，您将需要前往Products Page ，并为您要销售的任何商品创建新产品。

创建支付链接

付款链接允许您创建一个唯一的 URL 发送给买家，以便在您的仪表板上购买特定产品。 这就是我们将用来在我们的信息亭向感兴趣的买家收取款项的方式。

要创建付款链接，请导航到 Stripe 仪表板中的产品页面，然后选择您要为其创建付款链接的产品。

在您的产品页面上，您将看到与您的帐户关联的所有产品的列表。

在产品页面的定价部分下，查找显示创建付款链接的按钮。

按照此向导中的步骤创建一个 URL 以支付产品费用。 您可以单击预览窗格上的移动电话图标，以查看您的客户在从移动设备加载此 URL 时会看到什么。

填写完付款链接的详细信息后，单击“创建付款链接”。 这里的所有都是它的！ 下一页是一个 UI，可让您复制一个唯一的 URL 来支付该产品的费用。

暂时保持此选项卡打开 - 我们将在下一步中使用此页面上的 URL。

将支付链接 URL 保存到 NFC 标签

如果您使用过带有 Apple Pay 的 iPhone 或带有 Google Pay 的 Android 设备，那么您之前使用过 NFC。 它与让您在收银机上点击付款的功能相同。

我以大约 15 美元的价格购买了一包 50 个 NFC 标签 - 它们可以从各种供应商处轻松获得，并且具有多种不同的外形尺寸。 我购买的是薄的圆形塑料圆盘，直径约为一英寸。 我还看到 NFC 作为贴纸出售，或者嵌入印刷媒体（如名片或海报）中。

对于这个用例，我们将编写一个指向 NFC 标签的 URL，因此当客户用手机点击它时，他们手机的本地浏览器将导航到标签上的网址。 如果您使用的是新的空白标签，则该过程相当简单：您将下载一个移动应用程序，并使用它为您的标签编写一个 URL。 我使用 Play Store 中名为NFC Tools的免费应用程序（ Apple App Store上的 IOS 也可以使用）写入我的 NFC 芯片。 目前，我使用 Pixel 5 作为我的主要手机 - 我选择了 NFC 工具，因为它在 Play 商店中获得了很高的评价，并且非常适合这项工作！

使用 NFC 工具应用程序将 URL 写入 NFC 芯片的步骤：

  1. 在写入选项卡上，点击“添加记录”
  2. 点击URL / URI按钮
  3. 将支付链接的 URL 粘贴到输入框中，然后点击 OK
  4. 该应用程序将提示您碰撞您的 NFC 标签- 现在是时候了！
  5. 将标签贴在手机上 - 当您看到绿色检查时，您就完成了！ 点击确定

不要忘记将 NFC 标签上的 READ ONLY 值设置为 true。 这将防止它被再次写入。 如果您不这样做，任何拥有手机的人都可以重新编程您的标签以指向网络上的任何 URL。

在写入 URL 后，使用 NFC 工具应用程序将 NFC 芯片设置为只读的步骤：

  1. 导航到其他选项卡
  2. 点击锁定标签
  3. 点击是确认警告
  4. 出现提示时，将 NFC 芯片与手机碰撞
  5. 当您看到绿色检查时，您的标签现在是只读的！ 点击确定

全部加起来

一旦您将支付链接编程到您的 NFC 芯片中，您就可以接受亲自购买了！ 现在，我们的买家不再需要一系列复杂的指令来接受付款，只需将他们的手机轻触自助服务终端上的 NFC 芯片，然后使用熟悉的结账流程在手机上付款。 在您的 Stripe Dashboard 中启用的任何付款方式都可以使用。 如果您有多个产品类型，您也可以为每种购买类型设置 NFC。

回报：跟进购买

这种付款流程不仅比其他方式更简单，而且还让您有机会在客户完成购买后与他们建立联系。 这是一件非常有价值的事情 - 以前从您那里购买过的人更有可能再次购买，并且您可以在 Stripe Dashboard 中获得有关您的客户的信息，您可以重新与当您有新产品要出售时。 这可以是第二天的短信或电子邮件，感谢他们的购买，要求他们在社交媒体上关注您，或者注册您的邮件列表。 您还可以使用带有 CRM 工具的仪表板来跟踪他们的购买历史并查看他们是否是回头客。

或者，使用二维码进行操作

如果您在无法设置 NFC 芯片的地方销售商品，或者您对潜在客户是否愿意用手机进行支付有些犹豫，您可以使用 QR 码达到同样的效果. 如果由于某种原因 NFC 标签不适合您的用例，您可以从 Stripe Dashboard 生成 QR 码。 为此，请访问任何付款链接的详细信息页面。 在那里，您会看到一个方便的二维码按钮：

单击该按钮将为您的产品的付款链接生成一个二维码，可以出于相同目的下载和打印该二维码。 当买家扫描您的二维码时，他们将被带到付款流程，就像上面一样。

一些注意事项

  • 付款链接非常适合像这样的简单购买，但在客户可能同时购买产品组合的情况下并不理想。 使用Paument Links 上的可选交叉销售功能可以在一定程度上缓解这种情况。 否则，如果您的客户在您的售货亭停留时想要购买多种类型的产品，则他们需要完成每种产品的购买工作流程。 此外，在结账时，客户可以勾选一个框以保存他们的购买信息，以便将来更快地购买，这可以使后续交易更容易。

勾选“保存我的信息”框可以让您的客户更快地进行后续交易

  • 关于使用测试模式的注意事项：当使用支付链接完成购买时，将在该购买旁边创建一个访客客户。 每张测试信用卡只会创建一位客人 - 这意味着在测试模式下，如果您一遍又一遍地使用同一张卡进行测试，您看到的客户可能会比您预期的要少。

概括

在这篇文章中，我们研究了一种使用带有 NFC 芯片的支付链接进行面对面购买的创造性方法。 这是一个简单的、无代码的解决方案，企业主可以利用它来提供快速、熟悉的结账体验。 如果你喜欢这个，你可能还想看看：

你怎么看？ 这是您可以用于您的业务的东西吗？ 我很想听听您如何使用支付链接、NFC 标签或二维码来简化销售。 如果有任何想法或问题，请在推特上标记我@irreverentmike ！



原文:

Payment Links are just that: links! Anywhere you can share a URL, you can create an opportunity to connect to paying customers. This blog post shares some ideas for in-person payments with NFC tags or QR codes.

NFC (Near-Field Communication) Tags are small, inexpensive circuits which can be used to store a small amount of information that can be scanned by any device with a compatible reader. Virtually every smartphone device available on the market today is NFC compatible.

Steps We'll Cover

  1. Create (or select) a product in the Stripe Dashboard
  2. Create a payment link for that product
  3. Copy the URL for that payment link, and write it to a QR code and NFC tag
  4. When someone bumps the tag with a smartphone, the payment workflow starts!

Background

A few weeks ago, I was visiting Key West on vacation. While walking around the quiet downtown area one afternoon, I happened upon a little kiosk in front of a house. At first glance, it looked a bit like a little free library - it was a simple little cabinet with a plexiglass window and a few small shelves in it. On each shelf was a beautifully designed postcard, designed and printed by a local artist.

The postcards were priced simply - $5 each, or 5 for $20. It was a trust-based system: the postcards were in an unlocked display, and to pay, I was asked to submit a payment with Venmo. I grabbed a stack of postcards, pulled out my phone, and followed the instructions displayed on the front of the kiosk to send $20 to the artist.

Very cool! I’m happy, and I’m sure the artist is happy, too! But – this experience got my entrepreneurial gears turning… I can’t help but feel like there’s lots of opportunities for the seller to do better here:

  • I had to follow a series of steps to pay, using a specific mobile payment app to send them payment. If I had never used that app before, this would create a moment of friction. It’s a lot of work to download an app, set up an account, put in my credit card number, etc
  • After I walked away with my purchase, the artist lost their opportunity to sell to me again, unless I happen to walk by the same kiosk and they’ve changed stock. Now that I’ve bought something from them once, I’m far more likely to do it again. What if the seller had a way to connect with me after the sale?

We can improve on this buying process using some tools from Stripe, without writing a single line of code! For this, we’ll use Payment Links - a handy way to create a URL which directs visitors to Stripe’s payment workflow for any of your products. This means our buyers don’t need to manually enter a phone number, username, or price to submit payment, leaving less room for error.

Prerequisites - Set up your Stripe account & Create a product

This tutorial assumes you’ve got a (free!) account set up with Stripe. If you don’t have one for your business already, you can register here in just a couple minutes. Once you’ve done that, you’ll want to head over to the Products Page, and create a new product for any items you’re looking to sell.

Create a Payment Link

Payment Links allow you to create a unique URL to send to buyers, for the purchase of specific products on your dashboard. This is what we’ll use to collect payments from interested buyers at our kiosk.

To create a payment link, navigate to the Products page in your Stripe Dashboard, and select the product you want to make a payment link for.

On your Products page, you’ll see a list of all products associated with your account.

On the product page, under the Pricing section, look for a button that says Create Payment Link.

Follow the steps in this wizard to create a URL to pay for the product. You can click on the mobile phone icon on the preview pane to see what your customers will see when they load this URL from their mobile device.

Once you’ve filled out the details of your payment link, click “Create Payment Link”. That’s all there is to it! On the next page is a UI which lets you copy a unique URL to pay for that product.

Keep this tab open for now - we’ll use the URL on this page in the next step.

Save the Payment Link URL to an NFC tag

If you’ve used an iPhone with Apple Pay or an Android Device with Google Pay, you’ve used NFC before. It’s the same functionality that allows you to tap-to-pay at a cash register.

I purchased a pack of 50 NFC tags for about $15 USD - they’re readily available from a variety of suppliers, and in loads of different form factors. The ones I purchased are thin, circular plastic discs, and are about an inch in diameter. I’ve also seen NFCs available for sale as stickers, or embedded in printed media like business cards or posters.

For this use case, we’re going to write a URL to the NFC tag, so when a customer taps their phone against it, their phone’s native browser will navigate to the web address on the tag. If you’re working with a new, blank tag, the process is fairly simple: you’ll download a mobile app, and use it to write a URL to your tag. I used a free app from the Play Store called NFC Tools (also available for IOS on the Apple App Store) to write to my NFC chip. At the moment, I use a Pixel 5 as my main phone - I chose NFC tools because it was highly rated in the Play Store, and it worked perfectly for this job!

Steps to write a URL to your NFC chip using the NFC tools app:

  1. On the Write tab, tap “Add a record”
  2. Tap the URL / URI button
  3. Paste the URL for your payment link in the input box, and hit OK
  4. The app will prompt you to bump your NFC tag - now is the time!
  5. Hold the tag against your phone - when you see the green check, you’re all done! Hit OK

🚨 WARNING: Set your chip to read only! 🚨

Don’t forget to set the READ ONLY value on the NFC tag to true. This will prevent it from being written to again. If you don’t do this, anyone with a phone can reprogram your tag to point to any URL on the web.

Steps to make your NFC chip read-only using the NFC tools app, after you’ve written a URL to it:

  1. Navigate to the Other tab
  2. Tap Lock tag
  3. Acknowledge the warning by hitting Yes
  4. When prompted, bump your NFC chip with your phone
  5. When you see the green check, your tag is now read only! Hit OK

Adding it all up

Once you've programmed a Payment Link into your NFC chip, you're ready to accept in-person purchases! Now, instead of a series of convoluted instructions to accept payments, all our buyers need to do is tap their phone against an NFC chip on the kiosk, and pay on their phone using a familiar checkout flow. Any payment methods that are enabled in your Stripe Dashboard will work. If you have more than one product type, you can set up an NFC for each type of purchase, too.

The payoff: follow-up on purchases

Not only is this payment process a bit more straightforward than the alternative, it also gives you the opportunity to connect with your customers after they complete their purchase. This is a super valuable thing to be able to do - people who have bought from you before are far more likely to do it again, and with the information you’ll have about your customers in your Stripe Dashboard, you can re-engage with them when you have got new products to sell. This can be a text or email the next day thanking them for their purchase, asking them to follow you on social media, or perhaps signing up for your mailing list. You can also use the dashboard with CRM tools to track their purchase history and see if they’re repeat customers.

Alternately, Do it with a QR code

If you’re selling things in a place where it isn’t practical to set up an NFC chip, or if you have some hesitation about your potential customers’ comfort tapping their phone to pay, you can achieve this same effect with a QR code. If for some reason NFC tags don’t fit your use case, you can generate a QR Code from the Stripe Dashboard. To do this, visit the detail page for any Payment Link. There, you’ll see a handy QR Code button:

Clicking that button will generate a QR code for your product’s payment link, which can be downloaded and printed for the same purpose. When buyers scan your QR code, they’ll be brought to the payment flow, just as above.

Some caveats

  • Payment Links are great for simple purchases like this, but not ideal in cases where customers may be purchasing combinations of products at the same time. This can be mitigated somewhat using the optional Cross-sells feature on Paument Links. Otherwise, if your customer wanted to buy more than one type of product when they stopped at your kiosk, they’d need to complete the purchase workflow for each product. Additionally, during checkout customers can tick a box to save their purchase information for quicker future purchases, which can make subsequent transactions easier.

Ticking the “Save my info” box makes subsequent transactions much faster for your customers

  • A note about using Test mode: When a purchase is completed with payment links, a Guest Customer will be created alongside that purchase. Only one guest will be created per test credit card - which means that in test mode, you may see fewer customers than you expect if you’re testing with the same card over and over.

Summary

In this post, we took a look at a creative way to use Payment Links with NFC chips for in-person purchases. It’s a straightforward, no-code solution that business owners can take advantage of to provide a quick, familiar checkout experience . If you enjoyed this, you may also want to check out:

What do you think? Is this something you’d be able to use for your business? I’d love to hear how you might use Payment Links, NFC tags, or QR Codes to make sales a bit easier. Tag me on twitter @irreverentmike with any thoughts or questions!
item.author: irreverentmike

item.title: 如何缓解怀孕期间睡觉时的背痛 | How to Relieve Back Pain During Pregnancy While Sleeping
item.description:
发表于如果您在怀孕期间遭受背痛，您可能想知道如何缓解睡眠时的不适。 有几种方法可以帮助您解决这个问题，包括锻炼、伸展运动和药物治疗。 幸运的是，很多女性都找到了解决背部问题的完美方法！ 除了这些补救措施外，市场上还有许多孕妇腰带可以帮助您减少睡眠时的不适。 咨询印度顶级脊柱外科医生。

锻炼

温和的身体活动可以帮助您加强背部，同时缓解怀孕期间的背部疼痛。 尝试水上运动或步行。 去看物理治疗师，学习一些温和的背部伸展运动。 尝试在拉肚子的同时躺在你的手和膝盖上。 如果您无法伸展背部，请坐在地板上并绕着背部。 物理治疗师可以为您演示这些伸展运动。 怀孕按摩是另一个不错的选择。 拉伸 许多孕妇在怀孕期间抱怨背痛。 对于大多数女性来说，这种疼痛常常被忽视，但重要的是要保持运动以预防或缓解怀孕期间的背痛。 定期进行伸展运动将减少背痛的可能性并改善您的日常活动。 请继续阅读，了解孕妇在睡觉时可以做的一些最佳伸展动作，以缓解背部疼痛。 药物治疗 虽然不建议大多数孕妇服用止痛药，但对乙酰氨基酚是安全的。 但是，您应该在服用任何药物之前咨询您的医生，包括非甾体抗炎药 (NSAID)。 肌肉松弛剂也是如此。 您的医生会根据您的病情推荐安全的药物和剂量。 重要的是要记住，怀孕期间的背痛是怀孕的常见症状，如果您遇到这种并发症，就医是很重要的。 孕妇腰带 孕妇腰带是支撑腹部并防止下背部拉伤的支撑内衣。 它们可以穿在衣服外面或里面。 虽然不完全是一个可爱的发明，但它们可以在怀孕期间提供很大的帮助。 孕妇在坐着和站着时可能倾向于无精打采。 通过纠正他们的姿势，他们可以减轻下背部的压力并增强他们的核心肌肉。 热或冷

孕妇可以通过多种方式缓解背痛。 其中一些包括冰袋、热敷或冷敷。 怀孕荷尔蒙也会改变重心，这会导致背痛。 不良的姿势、站立和弯腰都会导致背部疼痛。 此外，骨盆区域的变化会导致背痛。 下面列出了一些技巧，可帮助您缓解怀孕期间睡觉时的背痛。

孕妇枕

产妇枕头用于在睡眠时提供支撑和舒适感，它们可以在多种方面发挥作用。 您可以将它们放在双腿之间以获得额外支撑，或平躺在腹部以缓解背部疼痛。 不过，这些枕头不仅适用于孕妇。 有背部或膝盖问题的人也可以使用它们。 有些女性甚至在长途旅行中在车上使用它们。 这些枕头也有助于产后恢复。

脊柱外科医生可以为您治疗。

阿卜杜拉·阿德尔 (AB) - 伊曼纽尔·巴托莱西 - Apr 8 4月8日



原文:

Sattvik Spine & Scoliosis Center

Posted on May 4

If you suffer from back pain during pregnancy, you may be wondering how to ease the discomfort while you sleep. There are several options to help you with this problem, including exercise, stretching, and medication. Luckily, a lot of women have found the perfect solution to their back problems! In addition to these remedies, there are also many maternity belts on the market that can help you minimize your discomfort while you sleep. Get consulted with Top Spine Surgeon in India.

Exercise

Gentle physical activity can help you strengthen your back while relieving back pain during pregnancy. Try water exercise or walking. See a physical therapist to learn some gentle back stretches. Try lying on your hands and knees while pulling in your belly. If you can't stretch your back, sit on the floor and round your back. A physical therapist can demonstrate these stretches for you. A pregnancy massage is another great option. Stretching Many pregnant women complain of back pain during the course of their pregnancy. This aches and pains often goes unnoticed for the majority of women, but it is important to keep up with exercise to prevent or relieve back pain during pregnancy. Practicing stretching exercises regularly will reduce the likelihood of back pain and improve your daily activities. Read on for some of the best stretches for pregnant women to do to ease back pain while sleeping. Medication While most pregnant women are not advised to take painkillers, acetaminophen is safe. However, you should consult with your doctor before taking any medication, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs). The same goes for muscle relaxants. Your doctor will recommend a safe medication and dosage for your condition. It is important to remember that back pain during pregnancy is a common symptom of pregnancy, and it is important to seek medical attention if you are experiencing this complication. Maternity belts Maternity belts are supportive undergarments that hold the belly up and prevent lower back strain. They can be worn outside or inside clothing. Although not exactly a cute invention, they can be a great help during pregnancy. A pregnant woman may tend to slouch during sitting and standing. By correcting their posture, they can relieve pressure on the lower back and strengthen their core muscles. Heat or cold

A pregnant woman can relieve back pain in several ways. Some of these include ice packs, heat, or cold compresses. Pregnancy hormones also change the center of gravity, which can cause back pain. Poor posture, standing, and bending over all contribute to back pain. Additionally, changes in the pelvic area can lead to back pain. Listed below are some tips to help you relieve back pain during pregnancy while sleeping.

Maternity pillows

Maternity pillows are used to provide support and comfort while sleeping, and they can be useful in several ways. You can place them between your legs for extra support, or lie on your belly to ease back pain. These pillows aren't just for pregnant women, though. People with back or knee problems can use them as well. Some women even use them in the car during long trips. Postpartum recovery is also helped by these pillows.

spine Surgeon available for your treatment.

Abdullah Adeel (AB) - Mar 19

Emanuele Bartolesi - Apr 8
